title: Preparation of TET Buffer
author: Nicola O'Reilly
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfeujjew
materials: Please select between the recipes for 2 L TET and 5 L TET .

steps.title: Add 1800 mL, RNase free Water...
steps.instruction: Add 1800 mL, RNase free Water to a 2 L single use cell culture bottle.

steps.title: Make up to 2000 ml Water (RNAse free)
steps.instruction: Make up to 2000 ml Water (RNAse free). Swirl very well to ensure that Tween-20 is well mixed in.

steps.title: Label with batch number and date
steps.instruction: Label with batch number and date.

steps.title: Store TET buffer at solution at...
steps.instruction: Store TET buffer at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).

steps.title: Add 4500 mL, RNase free Water...
steps.instruction: Add 4500 mL, RNase free Water to a 5 L single use cell culture bottle.

steps.title: Make up to 5000 ml Water (RNase free)
steps.instruction: Make up to 5000 ml Water (RNase free). Swirl very well to ensure that Tween-20 is well mixed in.

steps.title: Label with batch number and date
steps.instruction: Label with batch number and date.

steps.title: Store TET buffer at solution at...
steps.instruction: Store TET buffer at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
